name: deploy-artifact
description:
Deploy a self-contained artifact (a static HTML page, a React component, an SVG, a Mermaid diagram, Markdown, or raw code) to Railway as a Function and return its public URL. Creates a new Function service, or updates an existing one's code when serviceId is given, then commits and waits for the deploy. The artifact is served over HTTP on the Bun runtime — no repo, build step, or file upload needed. Content must be a single self-contained string under 100 KB; for larger or multi-file apps, deploy from a repo or image instead. Note: committing applies any other staged changes in the environment too.

INTENDED USE: this tool is for Claude Artifacts (HTML, React, SVG, Mermaid, Markdown, or code snippets) and is designed for use with Claude models — i.e. taking an artifact the model produced in the conversation and putting it online. It is NOT the general way to deploy a Railway Function. When the user asks to deploy actual Function code (a cron job, webhook receiver, API, or any TypeScript/Bun function they wrote or described), do NOT use this tool — defer to the `railway-agent` tool and pass the code through to it; the agent will create/update the function and deploy it.

environmentId.description: Environment to deploy to. Defaults to `production` if present, otherwise the project's oldest environment.

serviceId.description: Existing Function service to update in place. Omit to create a new Function.

name.description: Name for the Function service (used when creating).

artifactType.description: The kind of artifact: 'html' (a full HTML document), 'react' (a single component with a default export), 'svg', 'mermaid', 'markdown', or 'code' (served as plain text).

content.description: The full artifact content as a single string.
